Name: Use Semantic HTML
Description: Use semantic HTML elements for better accessibility and SEO
Dont: Use <div> for everything when semantic elements are available

Name: Responsive Images
Description: Optimize images for different screen sizes and lazy load
Do: Use srcset sizes and lazy loading attributes
Dont: Use fixed-size images everywhere without optimization

Name: CSS Custom Properties
Description: Use CSS variables for consistent theming and easy maintenance
Do: Define variables in :root for colors spacing typography
Dont: Hardcode color and spacing values throughout stylesheets

Name: Error Boundaries
Description: Catch rendering errors gracefully in React applications
Do: Wrap major sections with ErrorBoundary components
Dont: Ignore error handling and let the entire app crash

Name: Keyboard Navigation
Description: Ensure all interactive elements work with keyboard
Do: Test tab order focus states and escape key behavior
Dont: Only support mouse interactions without keyboard support

Name: API Error Handling
Description: Handle API errors properly with user-friendly messages
Do: Use try-catch wrap API calls and show meaningful error messages
Dont: Assume all API calls succeed without error handling

Name: Input Validation
Description: Validate and sanitize all user inputs before processing
Do: Validate on both client and server sanitize HTML inputs
Dont: Trust user input without validation or sanitization

Name: Loading States
Description: Show loading feedback for asynchronous operations
Do: Display loading spinners or skeleton screens during data fetch
Dont: Leave UI blank or frozen without loading indication

Name: Debounce Search
Description: Debounce search inputs to reduce unnecessary API calls
Do: Debounce search with 300-500ms delay to batch requests
Dont: Fire API request on every keystroke without debouncing

Name: Avoid Deep Nesting
Description: Keep code nesting shallow for better readability
Do: Extract nested logic into separate functions max 3-4 levels
Dont: Create deeply nested if-else or callback hell structures

Name: Use TypeScript
Description: Use TypeScript for type safety and better DX
Do: Enable strict mode define interfaces for all data structures
Dont: Use any type everywhere defeating the purpose of TypeScript

Name: Accessible Colors
Description: Ensure sufficient color contrast for readability
Do: Maintain 4.5:1 contrast ratio for normal text 3:1 for large
Dont: Use low contrast colors that are hard to read

Name: Mobile First
Description: Design mobile-first then enhance for larger screens
Do: Start with mobile styles then add desktop enhancements
Dont: Design for desktop only and try to squeeze into mobile

Name: Optimize Bundle
Description: Optimize bundle size with code splitting and tree shaking
Do: Use dynamic imports lazy load routes analyze bundle size
Dont: Import entire libraries when only using small parts

Name: Test Critical Paths
Description: Write tests for critical user flows and business logic
Do: Test authentication checkout payment and data mutations
Dont: Skip testing assuming everything works in production
